tool.name: maskseq
tool.summary: Mask one or more explicit 1-based inclusive sequence intervals with molecule-aware default symbols

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `maskseq` masks explicit sequence intervals in each input record using the shared Epithema interval and sequence-edit foundations. User-facing coordinates are 1-based inclusive, while the implementation converts them into the core zero-based half-open interval model before applying in-place masking through the shared `mask_intervals` helper.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 tool accepts a local sequence file followed by one or more `start:end` intervals. FASTA, FASTQ, EMBL, and GenBank inputs follow the shared sequence IO path. Multiple intervals are allowed and are applied deterministically in place to every input record.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one masked sequence record per input record, preserving identifiers, descriptions, molecule kind, and other metadata. The default mask symbol is `N` for DNA, RNA, and currently unknown molecule kinds, and `X` for protein records. A custom mask character may be supplied when it is valid for the target record alphabet.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one historical-style `maskseq` interval-masking example in view and compares the Epithema FASTA payload against a committed expected output. The comparison validates the governed 1-based inclusive masking rule and default nucleotide mask symbol.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema maskseq`. Validation currently covers deterministic masking of an interior interval against a committed three-record FASTA fixture. Rust tests also cover whole-sequence masking, protein default masking with `X`, and rejection of biologically invalid explicit mask symbols.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: Intervals must be valid for every input record; Epithema does not clip partial overlaps silently. Empty intervals are not representable because coordinates are 1-based inclusive with `start <= end`. Custom mask characters are conservative: they must be compatible with the record alphabet, so a protein mask cannot use `N` and a DNA mask cannot use protein-only symbols.

artifacts.label: Three-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA fixture used for deterministic maskseq validation.

examples.title: Mask positions 2 through 3 in each record
examples.description: Applies the same 1-based inclusive interval to each record in a three-record FASTA fixture using the default nucleotide mask symbol.

expected_outputs.label: Masked sequences
expected_outputs.description: Each output record has positions 2 through 3 replaced with `N`, while identifiers and metadata are preserved.
